# /images — drop your generated files here

The site's HTML already references these exact filenames. Just place your
21 generated images in this folder using these names (case-sensitive) and
they'll appear on the site immediately — no further code changes needed.

home-hero.jpg
home-about-teaser.jpg
home-approach.jpg
about-support-workers.jpg
services-mental-health.jpg
services-behaviour-support.jpg
services-community-access.jpg
services-risk-management.jpg
services-24-7-support.jpg
services-domestic-support.jpg
team-registered-manager.jpg
team-deputy-manager.jpg
team-pbs-lead.jpg
team-senior-support-workers.jpg
team-support-workers.jpg
team-compliance-lead.jpg
team-training.jpg
blog-choosing-provider.jpg
blog-person-centred-care.jpg
blog-supporting-loved-one.jpg
404-notfound.jpg

See AlphaGlobe_Image_Prompts.md for the prompt used to generate each one,
and the required aspect ratio (the CSS will crop to fit automatically via
object-fit: cover, but starting with the right ratio gives the best result
and avoids awkward cropping of faces).

Note: this file itself (README.md) is not referenced anywhere and can stay
in this folder harmlessly, or be deleted once you've uploaded your images.
